description: Canonical memory policy for the Cici instance. Defines what to capture, how long to keep it, when to promote it, and how to deduplicate it.

capture_policy.description: Categories of thought that merit a Supabase write. All other input is ephemeral and should not be captured.
capture_when: Recurring habit or tracking entry (e.g., protein intake, exercise, sleep)
Active project context: decisions, blockers, open questions
Personal preference or intention that should persist across sessions
Insight or reflection explicitly worth retaining
BrewMind partner or business context verified at Tier A or B
do_not_capture: One-off queries or debugging questions with no recurring relevance
Model-generated summaries not verified by Xavier (Tier C)
Supabase search results or recent_thoughts cited as fact
Transient session scaffolding (e.g., planning steps, intermediate notes)

durability_classes.description: Every capture must declare a durability class at write time. This is the primary signal used by memory-auditor to classify, promote, or expire entries.

transient.description: One-off, session-scoped data. No recurring relevance. Examples: a bug question, a quick lookup, a one-time calculation.

persistent.description: Recurring or pattern-based data. Should survive across sessions. Examples: twice-daily protein tracking, active project state, personal preferences.

merge_when: Same topic, same durability_class, captured within 24 hours
Semantic similarity score >= 0.90 on the same tag set
keep_separate_when: Same topic but different durability_class — flag both for manual review
Similar content but different tags — keep, do not auto-merge
flag_for_review_when: Two persistent entries contradict each other on the same topic

weekly.action: archive expired transient entries, report count

monthly.action: full audit: flag persistent entries approaching expiry, identify synthesis candidates, report Tier C leaks

tag_taxonomy.description: Canonical tag vocabulary. evidence-stager and memory-auditor must use only these tags when classifying entries.

tags.#habit: Recurring personal habit or tracking entry (health, fitness, nutrition)
tags.#decision: An explicit decision made by Xavier — not model-suggested
tags.#insight: A reflection or realization worth retaining across sessions
tags.#preference: A personal preference or working style note
tags.#brewmind: Any entry related to the BrewMind project or its partners
tags.#work: Professional or technical context unrelated to BrewMind

tag_taxonomy.unknown_tags: flag for taxonomy review — do not silently drop

source_weighting.description: When two sources conflict on the same fact, this hierarchy resolves the conflict. Higher = more authoritative.

hierarchy.notes: Structured synthesis of evidence. Wins over raw captures.
Staged raw imports. Wins over runtime.
Operational memory. Never overrides governed-state without a proposal.
Lowest trust. Must be verified before use.

source_weighting.conflict_action: annotate with Tension: label, log in brewmind-open-loops.md, surface to Xavier for resolution
